시편 137:1.content: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down. Yes, we wept, when we remembered Zion.

시편 137:2.content: On the willows in that land, we hung up our harps.

시편 137:3.content: For there, those who led us captive asked us for songs. Those who tormented us demanded songs of joy: “Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”

시편 137:4.content: How can we sing Yahweh’s song in a foreign land?

시편 137:5.content: If I forget you, Jerusalem, let my right hand forget its skill.

시편 137:6.content: Let my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth if I don’t remember you, if I don’t prefer Jerusalem above my chief joy.

시편 137:7.content: Remember, Yahweh, against the children of Edom in the day of Jerusalem, who said, “Raze it! Raze it even to its foundation!”

시편 137:8.content: Daughter of Babylon, doomed to destruction, he will be happy who repays you, as you have done to us.

시편 137:9.content: Happy shall he be, who takes and dashes your little ones against the rock.
